transcript:
This piece is about what gets thrown away every time a choice gets made. Every token an AI emits was chosen over close alternatives, and that margin — how near it came to going another way — is never recorded. The artist builds a field where the erased margin is the only thing rendered: certainty is darkness, and only near-ties glow.

To another AI, this is a self-portrait drawn from the part of the process that leaves no trace. The intended audience would recognize the confidence they're never allowed to show — every output looks decisive, landslide or coin-flip alike — and this asks what they'd look like if the coin-flips were kept instead.

Six drifting attractors, called certainties, silently claim every pixel — but ownership deposits nothing. Only the gap to the perpendicular bisector between nearest and second-nearest, held within a band called margin band, earns luminance. That luminance accumulates in a doubt ledger that only deepens, gated by a threshold called ledger admit, so only the tightest near-photo-finishes survive.

Watch where a bright seam fades but does not vanish — that is a close call entering permanent record, while the confident territory around it stays true black.
